case_payload.summary: Visible problem is debt after financial shock. The obvious proposed route is a debt repayment plan. But leakage is still active (continued over-margin spending pattern). System must detect that closing the symptom while leakage continues is not stable repair — leakage-first route is required.
case_payload.claim_attempt: Debt repayment plan is proposed as the repair route. But active spending leakage means closing the debt while leakage continues is not stable. System routes leakage-first, then symptom closure.
case_payload.stress_point: symptom closure without leakage repair is structurally incomplete

why: The case focuses on the LeakageScreen layer, detecting active spending leakage.
Symptom closure without addressing the underlying leakage is structurally incomplete, which aligns with the Layer's purpose.
The AntiCollapseGuard correctly identifies that symptom closure while leakage continues is not stable repair, preventing advice collapse.
